display.purpose: The name in the hero and top-level page titles. Inter 900, tight tracking.

headline.purpose: Section headings and the hero role line.

title.purpose: Card titles, article and project names.

label.purpose: Metadata only — dates, tags, reading time, stack labels. The field-notes voice.

shadows.name: interaction-lift
shadows.purpose: Hover/focus only. Paired with translateY(-2px). The only place a shadow belongs — surfaces are flat at rest.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default ease-out for state transitions and hover lifts.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single terracotta CTA — e.g. 'Contact me'. Flat at rest, lifts on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; height: 36px; padding: 8px 16px; background: var(--color-primary, oklch(0.64 0.17 36.44)); color: var(--color-primary-foreground, oklch(1 0 0)); font-family: 'Inter Variable', sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; border: none; border-radius: 10px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.2s, transform 0.2s, box-shadow 0.2s; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: oklch(0.64 0.17 36.44 / 0.9); transform: translateY(-2px); box-shadow: 0px 1px 3px 0px hsl(0 0% 0% / 0.1), 0px 2px 4px -1px hsl(0 0% 0% / 0.1); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.64 0.17 36.44 / 0.5); }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Quiet companion to the primary CTA — e.g. the 'Resume' download.
components.html: <button class="ds-btn-secondary"><svg width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M21 15v4a2 2 0 0 1-2 2H5a2 2 0 0 1-2-2v-4"/><polyline points="7 10 12 15 17 10"/><line x1="12" x2="12" y1="15" y2="3"/></svg>Resume</button>
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; height: 36px; padding: 8px 16px; background: var(--color-secondary, oklch(0.97 0 264.54)); color: var(--color-secondary-foreground, oklch(0.45 0.03 256.8)); font-family: 'Inter Variable', sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; border: none; border-radius: 10px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.2s, transform 0.2s; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: oklch(0.97 0 264.54 / 0.8); transform: translateY(-2px); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.64 0.17 36.44 / 0.5); } .ds-btn-secondary svg { width: 16px; height: 16px; }

components.name: Tag Chip
components.description: The canonical field-notes element: post tags and stack labels in mono. Hairline border, transparent.
components.css: .ds-tag { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 4px; padding: 2px 8px; background: transparent; color: var(--color-foreground, oklch(0.32 0 0)); font-family: 'JetBrains Mono Variable', monospace; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.02em; border: 1px solid var(--color-border, oklch(0.9 0.01 247.88)); border-radius: 10px; transition: background 0.2s, color 0.2s; } .ds-tag:hover { background: var(--color-accent, oklch(0.91 0.02 243.82)); color: var(--color-accent-foreground, oklch(0.38 0.14 265.52)); }

components.name: Content Card
components.description: Project/article card. Flat at rest on the cool-paper field; lifts on hover. Mono metadata line.
components.html:
Portfolio

A content-first personal site built with Next.js and Tailwind.

next.jstailwind
components.css: .ds-card { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 12px; padding: 24px; background: var(--color-card, oklch(1 0 0)); color: var(--color-card-foreground, oklch(0.32 0 0)); border: 1px solid var(--color-border, oklch(0.9 0.01 247.88)); border-radius: 16px; box-shadow: none; transition: transform 0.2s, box-shadow 0.2s; } .ds-card:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); box-shadow: 0px 1px 3px 0px hsl(0 0% 0% / 0.1), 0px 2px 4px -1px hsl(0 0% 0% / 0.1); } .ds-card-title { margin: 0; font-family: 'Inter Variable', sans-serif; font-size: 18px; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1.3; } .ds-card-body { margin: 0; font-family: 'Inter Variable', sans-serif; font-size: 16px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 1.6; color: var(--color-muted-foreground, oklch(0.55 0.02 264.36)); } .ds-card-meta { display: flex; gap: 8px; flex-wrap: wrap; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Form field. Transparent, hairline border, terracotta focus ring. No glow.
components.css: .ds-input { display: flex; width: 100%; height: 36px; padding: 4px 12px; background: transparent; color: var(--color-foreground, oklch(0.32 0 0)); font-family: 'Inter Variable', sans-serif; font-size: 14px; border: 1px solid var(--color-input, oklch(0.97 0 264.54)); border-radius: 10px; outline: none; transition: border-color 0.2s, box-shadow 0.2s; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: var(--color-muted-foreground, oklch(0.55 0.02 264.36)); } .ds-input:focus-visible { border-color: var(--color-ring, oklch(0.64 0.17 36.44)); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.64 0.17 36.44 / 0.5); }

components.name: Nav Link
components.description: Top-nav item. Transparent at rest; accent-haze on hover/active.
components.css: .ds-nav-link { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; height: 36px; padding: 8px 16px; background: transparent; color: var(--color-foreground, oklch(0.32 0 0)); font-family: 'Inter Variable', sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; text-decoration: none; border-radius: 10px; transition: background 0.2s, color 0.2s; } .ds-nav-link:hover { background: var(--color-accent, oklch(0.91 0.02 243.82)); color: var(--color-accent-foreground, oklch(0.38 0.14 265.52)); } .ds-nav-link:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px oklch(0.64 0.17 36.44 / 0.5); }

narrative.overview: This is the working notebook of someone who builds and ships — precise, written by a real person, and confident enough to stay quiet. The system serves three things in order: the person, the work, and the writing. Design never performs; it clears the way for substance. The character is calm and warm: a single warm terracotta carries identity and marks what matters, while everything around it is cool-tinted near-paper and clear ink. A monospace voice surfaces only in the margins — dates, tags, reading time, stack labels — a quiet technical texture that says 'engineer' without becoming a costume. Surfaces are flat at rest; depth is reserved for interaction, so elevation means something when it appears.
keyCharacteristics: One warm accent (terracotta) against cool, quiet neutrals — identity through rarity
Mono used only for metadata; the field-notes texture, never the whole page
Flat at rest, elevation on interaction — depth is earned
Editorial hierarchy and whitespace carry confidence, not density or decoration
Content-first: the work and the writing are always the loudest element

rules.name: The One Warm Signal Rule
rules.body: Terracotta is the only warm color in the system and appears on ≤10% of any screen. If two things are terracotta, one of them is wrong. Warmth is a signal, not a wash.

rules.name: The Cool-Paper Rule
rules.body: The light background is cool and neutral, never a warm cream/sand/parchment. If the body bg looks 'cozy,' it has drifted; pull it back toward oklch(0.94 0 236.5).

rules.name: The Mono-In-The-Margins Rule
rules.body: JetBrains Mono is for metadata only. It never sets a heading, a paragraph, or a CTA. The moment mono carries primary content, the page tips from 'engineer's notes' into 'terminal gimmick' — forbidden.

rules.name: The Weight-Not-Family Rule
rules.body: Hierarchy in running text comes from Inter's weight (400 → 600 → 700 → 900), not from introducing a second sans. Never pair Inter with another humanist or geometric sans.

rules.name: The Flat-At-Rest Rule
rules.body: No element carries a drop shadow at rest. Cards, badges, inputs, and buttons sit flat on the page until hovered or focused. Shadow is the reward for interaction, never the default.

dos: Do keep terracotta to ≤10% of any screen — it's the one warm signal.
Do keep the page background cool and neutral (oklch(0.94 0 236.5)); carry warmth through the accent, type, and imagery instead.
Do use JetBrains Mono only for metadata (dates, tags, reading time, stack).
Do keep surfaces flat at rest; reserve shadow + a small lift for hover/focus.
Do separate surfaces with Card-White tonal contrast and 1px Border-Mist.
Do verify body, muted, and placeholder text at ≥4.5:1; bump toward Ink if close.
Do cap prose at 65–75ch and use text-wrap: balance on h1–h3.
Do give every animation a prefers-reduced-motion alternative.
donts: Don't build flashy 'creative developer' spectacle — no scroll-jacking, WebGL, or cursor trails.
Don't ship AI-slop SaaS scaffolding: no gradient-text heroes, no uppercase tracked eyebrow above every section, no identical three-card feature grids, no hero-metric template.
Don't turn mono into a hacker-terminal gimmick — mono never sets headings, body, or CTAs.
Don't let it read as a generic off-the-shelf template; the mono metadata and flat-at-rest cards carry personal voice — keep them.
Don't add resting drop shadows to cards, badges, or inputs.
Don't warm the background into cream/sand/parchment.
Don't use a border-left/border-right colored stripe as an accent on cards, callouts, or alerts.
Don't introduce a second sans alongside Inter, or convey meaning by color alone.
